techniques.title: Produce a list of factors to monitor for sustainability impacts

procedure.1: Identify all machine-generated factors (hardware/software, etc).
procedure.2: Identify all human-generated factors (business/user, etc).
procedure.3: Create a checklist to be published within sustainability processes.
procedure.4: Circulate among employees as part of the testing workflow.
procedure.5: Check that all factors have been accounted for before publication.
procedure.6: Report the findings within your sustainability statement.

techniques.title: Use third-party APIs to measure any passive external impact

procedure.1: Identify providers who will offer transparent PPP measurements.
procedure.2: Check the impact of utilization of third-party APIs (if possible).
procedure.3: Check the impact of non-users or passive users affected by your service.
procedure.4: Check that impacted users are accounted for in compliance checks.
procedure.5: Report the findings within your sustainability statement.

techniques.title: Use quantitative research to measure the needs of users and affected communities

procedure.1: Check to see if deliverables require internal access (if so, permission is given).
procedure.2: Gather metrics data through browser APIs, analytics, or third-party software.
procedure.3: Identify resolvable factors that are causing PPP issues.
procedure.4: Once validated as accurate, test further using non-machine testable qualitative methods such as A/B measurement or feedback forms.

techniques.title: Test against specific disability profiles for more calibrated accessibility

procedure.1: Identify users or individuals with disabilities who can help you become more accessible.
procedure.2: Create testable profiles from symptom/issue lists that cause issues.
procedure.3: Check that potential solutions do not interfere with existing functionality.
procedure.4: Create and implement newly calibrated accessibility features.
procedure.5: Check that the features work as implemented against all existing functionality.
procedure.6: Report the findings within your accessibility statement.

techniques.title: Measure interaction cost to reach every page in the information architecture

procedure.1: Check that the information architecture has been designed with clarity in mind.
procedure.2: Check the page for identifiable way-finding processes along a route such as breadcrumbs and landing regions/page patterns.
procedure.3: Check that the number of choices for each action does not exceed a set amount.
procedure.4: Check that every action is well documented and that the expected action occurs.
procedure.5: Check the user flow path for achieving set goals and optimize with shortcuts if necessary.

techniques.title: Provide a human-readable sitemap to improve the information architecture

procedure.1: Check for a sitemap.xml file in the base directory of a website.
procedure.2: Check that the markup of the file is semantically correct.
procedure.3: Check that all of the links held within do not produce any errors.
procedure.4: Check for the existence of a publicly visible HTML sitemap.
procedure.5: Check that the file exists alongside a sitemap.xml format.
procedure.6: Check that the HTML sitemap is semantically correct.

techniques.title: Request permission for invasive access to interact with content

procedure.1: Check which permissions from the available APIs are being granted.
procedure.2: Check if products or services have unsubscribe/account freezing and removal options.
procedure.3: Check if cookies are used and if so, if notifications are triggered appropriately (with the option to disable all).
procedure.4: Check if applications plug-in functionality and allow for customization.

techniques.title: Encourage features that help users achieve tasks

procedure.1: Check for read it later compatibility within articles.
procedure.2: Check for purchasable products and the option to quickly purchase (bonus points if no login is required).
procedure.3: Check if the HTML head contains landmark links of relevance to be identified.
procedure.4: Check for recognized landmark shortcuts (skip links, etc) and that they are functional.
procedure.5: Check the page's content to ensure none is clipped by other materials or an overflow issue (with a scrollbar lacking).
procedure.6: Check that all links and buttons are functioning.
procedure.7: Check that progressive disclosing content only appears when requested and is compatible with user's browsers.
procedure.8: Check that progressive disclosure is used appropriately with your content or tasks to perform.
procedure.9: Check that CSS and JavaScript overlays, custom scrollbars and cursors, popup events, and new window opening triggers are not actioned without user consent.
procedure.10: Check that decorative features, and interactive media, images, or animation can be disabled at the user's request.

techniques.title: Avoid mechanisms that promote excessive screen time

procedure.1: Check if infinite scrolling is being used through pagination landmark links.
procedure.2: Check for recognized attention-keeping patterns that obscure content.

techniques.title: Eliminate deceptive design patterns or manipulative techniques

procedure.1: Check for bad practices such as those mentioned in the examples.
procedure.2: Check against a library of [deceptive design](https://www.deceptive.design/) patterns.

techniques.title: Determine if analytics can be sustainably improved or removed

procedure.1: Check to determine if an analytics script is being used within the page.
procedure.2: Check against a list of suppliers of software to determine viable alternatives.
procedure.3: Check to determine if data being gathered complies with GDPR and other privacy legislation.
procedure.4: If no viable alternative exists and it still performs poorly, recommend removal.

techniques.title: Eliminate any manipulative SEO techniques from your source code

procedure.1: Check for the keywords META tag and recommend its removal.
procedure.2: Check for the refresh META tag and recommend replacement with a page redirect header.
procedure.3: Check for hidden content as identified in the above example and promote visibility or removal.
procedure.4: Check for duplicate content and flag it for removal.

techniques.title: Provide deliverables in reusable, self-isolated components

procedure.1: Check to see if deliverables require internal access (if so, permission is given).
procedure.2: Check to determine if these deliverables are housed in an open source environment.
procedure.3: Check projects for multiple CSS files (or use of @imports).
procedure.4: Check JavaScript for the use of imports or self-isolating functions.
procedure.5: Check documentation for section separation as markdown files.

techniques.title: Provide documentation to guide the deliverables

procedure.1: Check to see if deliverables require internal access (if so, permission is given).
procedure.2: Check that the instructions, manual, or documentation exist.
procedure.3: If the documentation does not exist, flag a request to produce materials.
procedure.4: Check for multiple format types such as HTML, PDF, checklists, slideshow, and video.
procedure.5: Check if complex features could use additional contextual help.
procedure.6: Check if interactive assistance or instruction should be offered.
procedure.7: If additional aids would provide sustainability benefits, offer them.

techniques.title: Create code comments to describe the functionality of features

procedure.1: Check for code comments within the source code of pages.
procedure.2: Check the formatting of comments for conventions such as links, images, and bullets.
procedure.3: If publicly facing, flag it for removal, if internal access is required or a developer release, keep it in place.

techniques.title: Implement an up-to-date design system

procedure.1: Check for the implementation of a design system.
procedure.2: Check what number of categories being monitored.
procedure.3: Check if the patterns and tokens are being employed.
procedure.4: Check when the design system was last updated.
procedure.5: Check for common in-page design patterns or libraries.
procedure.6: Verify page components and overall design against established conventions.
procedure.7: Check for and implement solutions for layout flow issues that could be optimized using an established pattern.

techniques.title: Remove any functionality that may trigger auto-playing animation

procedure.1: Check for background media and prevent auto-playing upon page load.
procedure.2: Check that media that is required to play as a function of the product is muted by default.
procedure.3: Check for audio and video HTML elements and remove any autoplay true events.
procedure.4: Check for custom audio or video players and flag auto-playing media.

techniques.title: Optimizing all audio and video assets for speed and compatibility

procedure.1: Check for compression potential against every available audio and video asset.
procedure.2: Provide optimized solutions if audio or video problems have been detected.
procedure.3: Check for third-party non-native media player usage and recommend native options (if possible).

techniques.title: Provide facades and lazy-loading for media, animated GIFs, and third-party content

procedure.1: Check for the position of the fold on a range of screen sizes and resolutions consumers use.
procedure.2: Check for HTML lazy loading attributes against all images falling outside this viewport.
procedure.3: Check for HTML lazy loading attributes against all images in progressive disclosure boxes.
procedure.4: Check that no video or audio elements trigger content upon the page load.
procedure.5: Check that any uses of the GIF extension are not animated by default.
procedure.6: Check that no impactful third-party content activate upon the page load.
procedure.7: Check if an in-page "Lo-Fi" or "Eco mode" option exists, if by choice, recommend by default.
procedure.8: Check for a low-fidelity/text-only/lightweight subdomain or path.
procedure.9: Check that the low-fidelity option is an agreed upon percentage lighter than the main website.

techniques.title: Optimizing all media assets for a variety of different resolutions

procedure.1: Check that the video assets are provided in a variety of common resolutions (which can be user-chosen).
procedure.2: Check for automatic detection and selection of media resolutions.
procedure.3: Check if clips or trailers are provided to give a shorter overview.
procedure.4: Check if downloads are provided for repeat viewing.

techniques.title: Add details about media impacts to the media management and use policy

procedure.1: Check for media management and use policy existence.
procedure.2: Check for a section on audio to verify impact coverage.
procedure.3: Check for a section on video to verify impact coverage.
procedure.4: Check for a section on media if the above does not exist to verify impact coverage.

techniques.title: Measure and respond to high-intensity animation usage

procedure.1: Check both CSS and JavaScript for animations and calculate the number that exists.
procedure.2: If too many (based on a justified value) exist, request the removal of the most impactful.
procedure.3: Check if animation techniques can be replaced by more efficient methods.
procedure.4: Check individual animations to determine if the CPU or GPU burden on devices is within safe limits.

techniques.title: Include a mechanism to pause or stop page animation

procedure.1: Check if the animation has a mechanism to pause or stop animation or effects.
procedure.2: Check that the buttons load and give a grace period to the animation taking place.

techniques.title: Provide accessible mechanisms around symbols in system fonts

procedure.1: Check that system fonts are replaced with accessible emojis.
procedure.2: Check that emojis are implemented with compatibility in mind.

techniques.title: Use modern, efficient custom typefaces within a set limit

procedure.1: Check that the number of custom typefaces does not exceed five.
procedure.2: Check that fonts are preloaded within the head of the HTML document.
procedure.3: Check that no obsolete font formats are listed (such as EOT, TTF, SVG).
procedure.4: Check that fonts are only provided using WOFF2 or WOFF as a fallback.
procedure.5: Check that if a custom font is variable enabled (refer to font a preferred), remove other references (bold, italic, etc).

techniques.title: Add a print-friendly media query or style sheet

procedure.1: Check that the website or application has a print-friendly media query (@print) or style sheet.
procedure.2: Check that the print CSS makes attribute content and URLs visible
procedure.3: Check that the print CSS considers content color accuracy and monochrome options
procedure.4: Check that the print CSS considers paper orientation, color, and size
procedure.5: Check that the print CSS hides unnecessary structural material

techniques.title: Optimizing all document assets across a variety of different formats

procedure.1: Check that the primary document format is the most browser-compatible.
procedure.2: Check that more than one format is given if a non-web format is offered for compatibility.
procedure.3: Check for compression potential against every available document asset.
procedure.4: Provide optimized solutions if document problems have been detected.

techniques.title: Provide pre-generated copies of repeat-use physical assets

procedure.1: Check that existing web assets are pre-generated for repeat-use.
procedure.2: Check code for asset compilation to identify if pre-generation would be better.

techniques.title: Provide document details including a URL within a page

procedure.1: Check that document pages do not embed large files (internally or with third-parties).
procedure.2: Check that document links are clearly described with information about name, format, language, size, and potentially a summary.
procedure.3: Check for choice options with format and language factors in documents.

techniques.title: Identify an affected party-focused testing and prototyping policy

procedure.1: Check if a testing policy is both published and publicly visible.
procedure.2: Check for key policy factors are presented within the text (optional).

techniques.title: Produce a list of checkpoints to ensure quality control

procedure.1: Check to see if deliverables require internal access (if so, permission is given).
procedure.2: Check that the testing regime is available (internally or externally).
procedure.3: Identify all bug and security tests to perform during the testing process.
procedure.4: Identify all web performance tests to perform during the testing process.
procedure.5: Identify all web accessibility tests to perform during the testing process.
procedure.6: Identify all web sustainability tests to perform during the testing process.
procedure.7: Report and resolve the findings within your auditing process.

techniques.title: Provide active monitoring for issues on a frequent schedule

procedure.1: Check that testing occurs hourly/daily/weekly to be considered active.
procedure.2: Check that all publicly visible pages are included within the monitoring.
procedure.3: Check that the facility providing the monitoring meets WSG.
procedure.4: Check that a report of ongoing listed issues is publicly available until fully resolved.
procedure.5: Provide resolutions for detected issues within a timely manner.

techniques.title: Establish performance testing routines with each new release

procedure.1: Check to see if deliverables require internal access (if so, permission is given).
procedure.2: Check that the testing regime is available (internally or externally).
procedure.3: Identify all web performance tests to perform during the testing process.
procedure.4: Report and resolve the findings within your auditing process.

techniques.title: Provide a publicly visible compatibility policy or statement

procedure.1: Check for a compatibility policy or statement's existence.
procedure.2: Check for sections on included and excluded coverage.
procedure.3: Check that a support method is offered for issues outside of scope.

techniques.title: Provide clarification when updates are significant over minor releases

procedure.1: Check for a publicly available change.log or release notes.
procedure.2: Check that the frequency of updates has not been less than 12 months.
procedure.3: Check that major releases provide details of breaking features.

techniques.title: Establish compatibility testing routines with each new release

procedure.1: Check to see if deliverables require internal access (if so, permission is given).
procedure.2: Check that the testing regime is available (internally or externally).
procedure.3: Identify all compatibility tests to perform during the testing process.
procedure.4: Check if additional legal compliance requirements are being met.
procedure.5: Report and resolve the findings within your auditing process.

techniques.title: Profile existing projects to identify common factors of value

procedure.1: Check the visual spacing of elements is not too cramped.
procedure.2: Check the scroll length of the document does not exceed a stated length.
procedure.3: Check the number of DOM nodes in the tree does not exceed a stated number.
procedure.4: Check the number of HTTP requests does not exceed a stated number.
procedure.5: Check if optimizing will be beneficial, if so, implement a solution.

techniques.title: Calculate the energy intensity of technologies for existing projects

procedure.1: Check the energy intensity of the HTML elements and attributes used.
procedure.2: Check the energy intensity of CSS at-rules, selectors, pseudo's, properties, and Values.
procedure.3: Check the energy intensity of JavaScript, DOM, CSSOM, and API usage.
procedure.4: Check the energy intensity of image and media usage.

techniques.title: Minify your front-end code if it is public facing

procedure.1: Check if your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript can be minified.
procedure.2: Check if JavaScript can gain size reductions through uglify processes.
procedure.3: Check if the code is on a non-production public-facing website.
procedure.4: Check if obfuscation exists, if not minify the source code.

techniques.title: Implement code-splitting where appropriate to reduce payloads

procedure.1: Check the size of packages to determine the benefit of code-splitting.
procedure.2: Check if CSS would benefit from being split into separate style sheets based on queries.
procedure.3: Check if JavaScript can be modularized and imported when required.
procedure.4: Check if obfuscation exists, if not code-split the source code.

techniques.title: Eliminate redundant code through coverage or tree shaking

procedure.1: Check CSS to determine if redundancy exists within your code.
procedure.2: Check packages to determine if redundancy exists within JavaScript.
procedure.3: Check if redundant code may be reintroduced, if not, remove it.

techniques.title: Write code for performance removing duplication

procedure.1: Check for energy inefficient naming methodologies or patterns in CSS.
procedure.2: Check for the absence of repeating code (DRY) within CSS.
procedure.3: Check for duplicate functionality or names within your JavaScript.

techniques.title: Determine and decision make upon third-party impacts

procedure.1: Check if any third-party components exist within the page or application.
procedure.2: Check that component against WSG for its sustainability compliance.
procedure.3: Check if high-impact third-party components offer self-hosted alternatives.
procedure.4: Check that self-hosted alternatives are used if provided.

techniques.title: Provide an import on interaction delay for third-party resources

procedure.1: Check if any third-party components exist within the page or application.
procedure.2: Check that it exists behind a click-to-load delay screen (that users must request).

techniques.title: Ensure that content is delivered through the most sustainable pathway

procedure.1: Check if the level of content being published is regular.
procedure.2: Check if features such as comments are a requirement (if so, can moderation be offered).
procedure.3: Check if the website already has an audience to give content visibility.
procedure.4: If so, check that the content is self-hosted on the first-party domain.

techniques.title: Provide the ability to identify and choose third-party components to load

procedure.1: Check that information is provided to indicate upon load to indicate preferences.
procedure.2: Check that the option to allow all, deny all, and select between providers is offered.
procedure.3: Check that information is provided identifying providers and describing the options carefully.
procedure.4: Check that preferences are remembered when navigating during the session.

techniques.title: Validate source code for semantic accuracy

procedure.1: Check if the HTML, CSS, and other source code contains resolvable errors.
procedure.2: Check that any errors have been successfully linted and corrected.

techniques.title: Eliminate optional code to reduce the rendering impact

procedure.1: Check that optional HTML elements that are unnecessary for rendering are removed.
procedure.2: Check that browser default styles are not repeated in the style sheets.

techniques.title: Replace non-standard code with suitable alternative syntax

procedure.1: Check that archaic proprietary specification additions are not included within the code.
procedure.2: Check that browser prefixes have been removed from CSS unless required for compatibility.
procedure.3: Check that no browser hacks exist unless they are required for compatibility.

techniques.title: Replace code that can instead use more efficient native APIs

procedure.1: Check if the benefits of a native browser control exceed creating a custom element.
procedure.2: Check if a native API exists that could replace custom calls, methods, or functions.
procedure.3: In either of these instances, if the answer is yes, check that the native option is used.

techniques.title: Set scripts to load either asynchronously or deferred

procedure.1: Check that all scripts are set to load with either the async or defer attributes unless necessary.
procedure.2: Check if CSS ([with the aid of JavaScript](https://www.holisticseo.digital/pagespeed/async-css/)) could benefit from asynchronous loading, and if so, do it.

techniques.title: Provide assets required at load with the correct delivery route

procedure.1: Check that preload, prefetch, and preconnect are used as appropriate in HTML documents.

techniques.title: Ensure that the required HTML elements are included

procedure.1: Check that the document includes a well-formed DocType.
procedure.2: Check that the page or application includes the required HTML elements.
procedure.3: Check that the page or application includes valid links to CSS and JavaScript assets (optional).
procedure.4: Check that the HTML includes the viewport META element for handheld devices.

techniques.title: Provide relevant metadata using a recognized scheme

procedure.1: Check that the HTML document contains a description META element of [between 50 and 150 characters](https://moz.com/learn/seo/meta-description).
procedure.2: Check that the HTML document contains additional metadata from a recognized scheme.
procedure.3: Check that the necessary social media metadata exists within the HTML head.

techniques.title: Ensure content is structured using Microdata

procedure.1: Check that content is correctly marked up using microdata, microformats, etc.
procedure.2: Check for external RDFa assets that may contain rich metadata.

techniques.title: Ensure CSS preference media queries are correctly applied

procedure.1: Check for preference queries and that the effect they produce is beneficial.
procedure.2: Check for manual override toggles or preferences within the page.
procedure.3: Check for alternative styles if paged media or scripting is disabled.

techniques.title: Test against network speeds and visual resolution breakpoints

procedure.1: Check that the website or application performs well on a variety of network speeds.
procedure.2: Check that the product or service visually does not break when resized at multiple window sizes.

techniques.title: Provide progressive enhancement feature testing within projects

procedure.1: Check that the website or application can function if JavaScript is disabled.
procedure.2: Check that the page is still functional if an older browser version with older JavaScript and CSS version is used.
procedure.3: Check that the product or service is still functional if CSS is disabled.
procedure.4: Check that the product or service is still functional if images or media are disabled.

tests.expectedResults: Expectation of the above is dependent on the task but should aim to be true unless necessary.

techniques.title: Configure your project around carbon-aware situations

procedure.1: Check if operations can be built to function using carbon-aware technologies or resources.
procedure.2: Check if operations can be processed or shifted to occur during less carbon-intensive periods.

techniques.title: Provide low-impact, delayed, or alternate methods of interaction

procedure.1: Check for low-impact interaction methods such as QR codes, smartwatch layouts, or voice assistant tooling.
procedure.2: Check for a speech style sheet for indicators to clarify pronunciation issues.
procedure.3: Check that your website or application functions correctly in a text-only environment.

techniques.title: Optimize a codebase through rewriting for performance

procedure.1: Check that existing code has been rewritten to be less process intensive, etc.
procedure.2: Check that any existing tooling is considered sustainable and/or performant.
procedure.3: Check if JavaScript frameworks can be replaced with others that are more performant.
procedure.4: If frameworks can be replaced, check that they are replaced.

techniques.title: Ensure JavaScript APIs are correctly applied

procedure.1: Check for JavaScript APIs and that the effect they produce is beneficial.
procedure.2: Check that the API has enough support to be progressively implemented.
procedure.3: Check that the implementation cost is not greater than the amount it offsets.
procedure.4: If the API is dependent on third-parties, ensure they are sustainable and resilient.

techniques.title: Identify and remove unused packages and dependencies

procedure.1: Check if a package.json has been submitted for identifying packages.
procedure.2: If so, use that. Otherwise, check the source code for potential orphan references.

techniques.title: Implement modularized or lightweight frameworks and libraries

procedure.1: Check the package size to determine at what size breaking into modular components should occur.
procedure.2: Check the function sizes to identify if refactoring to smaller functions should also occur.
procedure.3: Check if CSS libraries could be replaced with smaller alternatives or custom code.
procedure.4: Check if JavaScript frameworks could be replaced with smaller alternatives or native code.
procedure.5: If replacements can be made, then replacements should be introduced.

techniques.title: Ensure that dependencies are verified as up-to-date

procedure.1: Check if a package.json has been submitted for identifying packages.
procedure.2: If so, use that. Otherwise, check the source code for package version data.
procedure.3: Check that all packages are at least non-breaking (minor) current if not major release current releases.

techniques.title: Provide the expected website assets in your base directory

procedure.1: Check the base directory for a favicon in 16×16, 32×32, and 48×48 format.
procedure.2: Check the base directory for a well-formed robots.txt document.
procedure.3: Check the base directory for a well-formed opensearch.xml and sitemap.xml file.
procedure.4: Check the base directory for a well-formed site.webmanifest document.
procedure.5: Check that the links contained within these documents do not contain errors.

techniques.title: Provide beneficial plaintext website assets in the expected locations

procedure.1: If you have advertising, check that the base directory contains a well-formed ads.txt document.
procedure.2: Check that the base directory contains a well-formed security.txt and robots.txt document.
procedure.3: Check that the base directory contains a well-formed carbon.txt and humans.txt document (optional).

techniques.title: Encourage the use of more sustainable creation toolchains

procedure.1: Check for the tool used for creation and determine its sustainability.
procedure.2: If it has a low impact, continue. Otherwise, flag for replacement.

techniques.title: Test all third-party resources for sustainability impacts

procedure.1: Check if any third-party resources exist within the page or application.
procedure.2: If internal access is required to verify resource usage, grant it.
procedure.3: Check that component against WSG for its sustainability compliance.
procedure.4: Check if high-impact third-party resources offer self-hosted alternatives.
procedure.5: Check that self-hosted alternatives are used if provided.

techniques.title: Ensure the latest version of a syntax language is being used

procedure.1: Check that the version of a programming language being used is the latest.

techniques.title: Publicly disclose relevant sustainability metrics to users

procedure.1: Check that the provider has made available relevant hosting metrics publicly.
procedure.2: Check that the PUE, WUE, and CUE are each listed for each data center.
procedure.3: Check that individual servers provide live CPU, GPU, RAM, and data use tracking.
procedure.4: Check that logging of past values over time is provided for calculating emissions.

techniques.title: Ensure that the project is hosted on a sustainable platform

procedure.1: Check that the hosting provider has a sustainability statement.
procedure.2: Check that the hosting is powered by 100% low-carbon energy.
procedure.3: Check that the hosting platform does not use carbon credits to offset its energy requirements.
procedure.4: Check for recognized green initiatives that the platform may be subscribed to.
procedure.5: Check if sustainability-related keywords or claims are made.
procedure.6: Check that any claims made are verified with supporting evidence.

techniques.title: Ensure that the project uses a sustainable domain registrar

procedure.1: Check that the provider has made available relevant impact metrics publicly.
procedure.2: Account for factors that may affect domain impact (such as number of domains in use).
procedure.3: Check that logging of past values over time is provided for calculating emissions.

techniques.title: Provide caching responses to optimize page reloads

procedure.1: Check the cache-control header of each file has the correct length set.

techniques.title: Identify the potential for PWAs and other useful features

procedure.1: Check if cookies are in use and, whether they can be added, removed, or replaced.
procedure.2: Check if local data storage is being used and, whether it can be added, removed, or replaced.
procedure.3: Check if a PWA or other worker functions are in use and, whether they can be added or removed.
procedure.4: If a PWA exists, check for a well-formed manifest file, an icon, and semantically correct JavaScript.

techniques.title: Ensure that files are compressed correctly before serving

procedure.1: Check all text assets for content-encoding headers to verify compression takes place.
procedure.2: Check content-encoding headers work for not in-use formats for forward-compatibility efforts.
procedure.3: Check if the compression method used is the most optimal type available.

techniques.title: Compress all assets that can be optimized before CMS submission

procedure.1: Check that all assets are optimized for the web in size, format, and quality before publication.
procedure.2: If assets have been altered prior to publication, check users are pre-warned of any changes.
procedure.3: Check that the files published are the optimized versions (more than one may be necessary).
procedure.4: Check if a CMS will be necessary or useful and if so, ensure static assets are uploaded there.

techniques.title: Provide custom error pages to handle unexpected events

procedure.1: Check that the website or application has a custom set of pages to respond to errors.
procedure.2: Check that each error status type has its own page and unique situation handling.
procedure.3: Check that such pages provide signposting to resolve the error they encountered.
procedure.4: Check that existing links to navigation related pages remain fully functional.
procedure.5: Check that links to customer support are provided when solutions do not work.

techniques.title: Resolve all anchor references which trigger errors or redirects

procedure.1: Check that no anchor reference or in-page reference contains links that do not resolve (server errors).
procedure.2: Check that no anchor reference or in-page reference contains dead (erroneous) links.
procedure.3: Check that no anchor reference or in-page reference contains redirection links.
procedure.4: Check that links to external websites do not result in forbidden errors.

techniques.title: Check that pages have not been subjected to hijacking

procedure.1: Check links do not contain abnormalities such as significant or feature-critical external redirections.
procedure.2: Check pages do not contain tracking, mining, or advertising that has occurred suddenly.
procedure.3: Check pages do not contain closure, ransom, or hacking notices.
procedure.4: Check pages do not trigger sudden redirections to other domains.
procedure.5: Check the domain has not been included within a security firm or Google compromised lists.

techniques.title: Ensure that stored data has a defined and reasonable expiry date

procedure.1: Check the expiration date of locally stored data and if that date has exceeded, remove the data.
procedure.2: Check the expiration date of server-side stored data and if that date has elapsed, remove the information.
procedure.3: If no expiration date exists for client-side or server-side data, either add one or remove the data.

techniques.title: Deliver content using the correct, most secure protocol

procedure.1: Check that the product or service is being delivered over HTTPS rather than HTTP.
procedure.2: Check that alternative protocol usage is only done using secure means.

techniques.title: Provide distributed access to static resources via a CDN

procedure.1: Check if you only serve a local (single country) audience, if so, avoid CDN usage.
procedure.2: Check using analytics the average distribution of your product or service's users.
procedure.3: Check the existing location of static resources on the existing website.
procedure.4: If they exist on the first-party domain, calculate the sustainability value of using a CDN.
procedure.5: Check a hosting or CDN provider's locations to match as closely to your customers.

techniques.title: Ensure that the CDN and its chain of locations are sustainable

procedure.1: Check if the CDN provider has a sustainability statement in place.
procedure.2: Check if the CDN provider meets any sustainability compliance obligations.
procedure.3: Check that CDN against WSG for its sustainability compliance.
procedure.4: Check that the CDN supply network (nodes) are also as sustainable as the provider.

techniques.title: Avoid hosting dynamic resources or third-party scripts on the CDN

procedure.1: Check that your CDN only contains static website assets.
procedure.2: Check that JavaScript on CDNs is first-party hosted only.

techniques.title: Provide a review or expiration date on website content

procedure.1: Check that all articles (or content where material may go out-of-date) have a review or expiration date.
procedure.2: Check that content within the website (or potentially within long articles) can be filtered by classification.
procedure.3: Check that all classification patterns used on websites are repeated across all pages and clearly labeled.

techniques.title: Ensure that backup providers are classified as sustainable

procedure.1: Check if the backup provider has a sustainability statement in place.
procedure.2: Check if the backup provider meets any sustainability compliance obligations.
procedure.3: Check that backup provider against WSG for its sustainability compliance.

techniques.title: Provide a publicly visible sustainability and/or ethical policy statement

procedure.1: Check that a publicly visible sustainability statement exists.
procedure.2: Check that a Code of Ethics statement can be located.
procedure.3: Check that the sustainability statement contains details on PPP reports.
procedure.4: Check that the sustainability statement has concrete dates planned.
procedure.5: Check that the sustainability statement details legal compliance.
procedure.6: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Provide details of any beyond-the-scope sustainability features

procedure.1: Check for any third-party sustainability features beyond WSG scope.
procedure.2: Check that any such features are met in both guidelines and success criteria.
procedure.3: Check that no conflicts are caused with existing WSG compliance.
procedure.4: If conflicts do exist, prioritize WSG unless a higher classification of document exists (law, etc).

techniques.title: Check that an employee is assigned as the sustainability representative

procedure.1: Check for a named employee and their contact details on the sustainability statement.

techniques.title: Produce awareness raising resources on sustainability

procedure.1: Check that the product or service's social media accounts for sustainability hashtag postings.
procedure.2: Check a product or services blog for sustainability-related postings.
procedure.3: Check a product or services footer for a sustainability hub or resources area for users.

techniques.title: Calculate the environmental impact of your competitors

procedure.1: Check your own product or service against WSG for a baseline score.
procedure.2: Check competitor websites against WSG to identify where they sit sustainably.
procedure.3: Check again regularly over time to monitor potential changes that may occur.

techniques.title: Have a clearly defined set of sustainability goals

procedure.1: Check for the existence of a publicly visible sustainability statement.
procedure.2: Check that a journey or path is provided to introduce the subject.
procedure.3: Check that current carbon emissions data is provided for context.
procedure.4: Check that goals over a 1, 2, 5, 10, and beyond year plan are defined.
procedure.5: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Verify and showcase any third-party sustainability certifications

procedure.1: Check that any certifications are marked up so they can be machine-read.
procedure.2: Check that any certifications allow for validation to prove the authenticity of ownership.
procedure.3: Check that all verified certifications are weighted into the sustainability scoring process.

techniques.title: Check for any included disclosure reporting policies

procedure.1: Check that the business operates under a disclosure reporting policy.

techniques.title: Include public facing carbon reports on an annual basis

procedure.1: Check that a public-facing carbon report is published annually.
procedure.2: Check if an open format is provided, or as a fallback - PDF format.
procedure.3: Check that carbon reports are linked to from within a sustainability statement.
procedure.4: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Check for any compliance related disclosure documentation

procedure.1: Check if a CSRD report has been provided, if so, evaluate the data sustainability.

techniques.title: Provide a product or service maintenance agreement

procedure.1: Check that a product or service maintenance agreement exists.
procedure.2: Check that the content provides coverage for the product or service.
procedure.3: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Identify the iteration record of the website or application

procedure.1: Check if a website or application has a history of being updated.
procedure.2: Check that the frequency is less than 12 months since the last update.
procedure.3: Check (if access to a repository is provided) that more minor than major updates occur.
procedure.4: Check (if access to a repository is provided) that structure-over-style updates occur.

techniques.title: Clearly label changes using a release notes feature

procedure.1: Check that a changelog has been provided for each product or service.
procedure.2: Check that modifications are categorized by the type of change.
procedure.3: Check that significant changes such as new features are well documented externally.
procedure.4: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Align product or service compliance with the SDGs

procedure.1: Check if the product aligns with the SDGs and if not, identify ways to address shortcomings.

techniques.title: Provide a publicly visible and transparent supplier policy

procedure.1: Check that a publicly visible supplier policy exists on the website.
procedure.2: Check that information covering all three aspects of PPP are covered.
procedure.3: Check that third-party supplier against WSG for sustainability.
procedure.4: If the supplier has a poor impact record post-testing, reconsider usage.
procedure.5: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Publicly list the sustainability impact of any partnerships

procedure.1: Check that all partnerships of the product or service are listed online.
procedure.2: Check those partners against public lists of known greenwashers.
procedure.3: Check those partners against WSG for sustainability conformance.
procedure.4: If the supplier has a poor impact record post-testing, reconsider usage.

techniques.title: Ensure that the business is a living wage employer

procedure.1: Check for a listing in the living wage directory or a verifiable declaration.
procedure.2: Check the jobs page of a product or service to identify listed wages.
procedure.3: Check those wages against the Living Wage current rates.
procedure.4: If the wage is not shown or does not match, flag a potential issue.

techniques.title: Ensure that the business provides benefits for employees

procedure.1: Check the work, culture, career, and jobs pages for potential benefits.
procedure.2: Calculate values for such benefits based on their potential usefulness.
procedure.3: Check if the added value of such benefits is likely to benefit workers.

techniques.title: Provide links to the privacy policy and relevant legislation

procedure.1: Check that a publicly visible privacy policy on the website.
procedure.2: Check that the privacy policy includes sections on required legislation.
procedure.3: If in Germany, check that an Impressum document is available.
procedure.4: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Enable users to manage their accounts and data

procedure.1: Check that users can delete their accounts and data without human intervention.

techniques.title: Provide a website archive containing outdated or old versions

procedure.1: Check if content exists beyond 5-10 years on the website or application.
procedure.2: Check if the mentioned content is being accessed rarely rather than regularly.
procedure.3: Check (if both of the above are true) that a website archive holds this material.
procedure.4: Check that the mentioned archive is slimmed down to further reduce sustainability impacts.

techniques.title: Provide a publicly facing policy around emerging technologies

procedure.1: Check that a publicly facing policy exists around emerging technology usage.
procedure.2: Check (if such technology is used) to what extent and how much of an impact this is causing.
procedure.3: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Verify all partnerships and sponsors against WSG

procedure.1: Check that all partnerships and sponsors are publicly listed on the website.
procedure.2: Check that all listings are correctly marked up so they can be machine-read.
procedure.3: Check that all partnerships and sponsors are vetted against public lists of known greenwashers.
procedure.4: Check that all partnerships and sponsors are tested against WSG for sustainability.
procedure.5: If the supplier has a poor impact record post-testing, reconsider usage.

techniques.title: Verify and showcase any third-party sustainability projects

procedure.1: Check that all third-party projects are publicly listed on the website.
procedure.2: Check that all listings are correctly marked up so they can be machine-read.
procedure.3: Check that third-party projects are weighted into the sustainability scoring process.

techniques.title: Provide a sustainability and environmental budget

procedure.1: Check that any budget information provided by the user is in a valid format.
procedure.2: Check to see if the user has submitted a JSON budget with predefined labels and values.
procedure.3: Check that WSG conformance (against success criteria) matches or is lower than the budget.

techniques.title: Provide a performance budget for your product or service

procedure.1: Check that any budget information provided by the user is in a valid format.
procedure.2: Check to see if the user has submitted a JSON budget with predefined labels and values.
procedure.3: Check that the performance criteria match or is lower than the budget.

techniques.title: Benchmark the improvement in revised budget achievements

procedure.1: Check that any budget information provided by the user is in a valid format.
procedure.2: Check to see if the user has submitted a JSON budget with predefined labels and values.
procedure.3: Check the submitted information against previously submitted budgets.
procedure.4: Check that the new budgets are equal or tighter (smaller) targets than previously.
procedure.5: Check the website or application's budget analysis data against previous tests.
procedure.6: Check that the website or application meets budget requirements regularly.

techniques.title: Produce an open source policy and verify licensing

procedure.1: Check that a publicly facing open source policy exists on the website.
procedure.2: Check that the policy contains content about creation and contribution.
procedure.3: Check that the content is written in plain English for readability.

techniques.title: Identify contributions made to open source projects

procedure.1: Check a product or service's website for a link to a public repository.
procedure.2: Check for projects where group members are the source or contributor.
procedure.3: Check activity levels for open source work are consistent and not irregular.

techniques.title: Maintain transparent communication when problems occur

procedure.1: Check for backups that are stored in a read-only, recoverable state (internal access).
procedure.2: Check for a public-facing policy relating to disaster recovery and continuity.
procedure.3: Check for a system status page and that everything is running correctly.
